channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: The “Fox News Sunday” interview reflected a broader media blitz by presidential nominee Kamala Harris and Walz as the Democrats seek to garner public attention in the final 30 days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: Nearly a week after verbal stumbles in the only vice presidential debate, Democrat Tim Walz used his debut campaign appearance on a Sunday news show to try to fend off criticism of his stand on abortion rights and “own up” to past misstatements.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top US trade official sees progress in helping workers. Voters will decide if her approach continues
item.description: As the U.S. trade representative, Katherine Tai is legally required to avoid discussing this year’s presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: KJP slammed after Hurricane Helene over mixed messages on whether FEMA resources used for migrants
item.description: White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre is under fire for apparently contradicting herself regarding the use of FEMA funds for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's what two undecided Wisconsin voters are holding out for in the 2024 election
item.description: A small percentage of voters in battleground state Wisconsin are still undecided, but that sliver of voters could decide the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from AP's report on affordable housing disappearing across the U.S.
item.description: While Americans continue to struggle under high rents, as many as 223,000 affordable housing units could disappear in the next five years alone.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A year into the Israel-Hamas war, students say a chill on free speech has reached college classrooms
item.description: Campuses across the U.S. have been calmer in the wake of last spring’s protests, yet many are mired in a lingering unease.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden pledged to campaign hard for Harris. So far, he's been mostly a no-show
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On the last day of August, President Joe Biden was asked about his fall campaign plans. He promised a Labor Day appearance in Pittsburgh and said he would be “on the road from there on.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says small business disaster loan program will soon run out of money
item.description: President Joe Biden says Congress needs to shore up a small business disaster loans fund, and fast. Meanwhile, the response to Hurricane Helene has become a presidential election issue.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: In Michigan, Harris doesn't get hoped-for firefighters endorsement amid shifting labor loyalties
item.description: REDFORD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — It was the perfect place to welcome the endorsement of the firefighters union — a gleaming new firehouse in a blue-collar town just outside of Detroit in the key battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Trump campaigns send surrogates to Las Vegas to court middle class, Latinos
item.description: With a month until Election Day, Vice President Kamala Harris and former President Donald Trump sent their surrogates to Las Vegas this week to campaign for Nevada’s six electoral votes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed Capitol is sentenced to probation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed the U.S. Capitol with a mob of Donald Trump supporters was sentenced to probation instead of prison on Friday, as the federal courts reached a milestone in the punishment of Capitol rioters.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senators fear FEMA ‘entanglement’ with border crisis could hurt disaster response mission
item.description: Lawmakers are seeking answers from the Biden administration on whether an "entanglement" with the border crisis has affected FEMA's ability to respond to disasters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Former President Obama is set to hit the campaign trail for Vice President Harris this month, starting his blitz in the key battleground state of Pennsylvania next week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Tim Walz to make fundraising trip to California
item.description: (FOX40.COM) — Minnesota governor and Democratic Vice Presidential Candidate Tim Walz is expected to visit several California cities starting this weekend as part of campaign fundraising efforts. On Sunday afternoon Walz will speak in San Diego and Montecito before heading to Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Jacque Porter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gloria, Turner compete in San Diego mayoral race debate
item.description: The debate between San Diego Mayor Todd Gloria and police officer and challenger Larry Turner covered a wide range of issues from transportation, flood infrastructure failures, housing shortages and of course homelessness.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: Minneapolis mayor, City Council clash on homeless shelter funding
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council is launching an inquiry into the city administration’s financial reporting practices following a dispute over funding for a homeless shelter.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Kamala Harris is making an unusual pilgrimage today — to the birthplace of the GOP
item.description: Harris will travel Thursday to Ripon, Wisconsin — home to the Little White Schoolhouse, where an 1854 meeting led to the birth of the Republican Party. She'll be joined by former GOP Rep. Liz Cheney.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Trump calls Jack Smith filing 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: FOX59/CBS4 Indiana Governor Debate
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS -- For the first time this election year, the two major party candidates are having the opportunity to participate in a televised debate surrounding issues that are important for Indiana voters. U.S. Senator Mike Braun, R-Ind.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Here are some important election deadlines for the Las Vegas valley
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Nevada voters have until Oct. 22 to register to vote should they want a mail-in ballot, and remember, in-person registration is available on Election Day.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge unseals new Trump Jan. 6 election interference evidence
item.description: Prosecutors provided the most detailed look yet at their election interference case against former President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Kamala Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: “This strike is about fairness. Foreign-owned shipping companies have made record profits and executive compensation has grown,” Harris said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Harris backs striking port workers, knocks Trump
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris voiced her support Wednesday for striking port workers while bashing her rival former President Donald Trump after tens of thousands of longshoremen at ports along the East Coast and Gulf of Mexico walked off the job.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: What we gleaned from key moments of the Walz-Vance debate in New York
item.description: MPR News senior politics reporter Dana Ferguson is in New York with takeaways from the only debate between the vice presidential hopefuls.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Dana Ferguson, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Soomaalida St. Cloud waxay rabaan in ay ka dhex muuqdaan siyaasadda, laakiin iyaga oo dhan isku aragti ma aha
item.description: In ka badan labaatameeyo sano ka hor ayey qaxootigan oo ka soo jeeda Bariga Afrika soo dageen St. Cloud, oo qaarkood ayaa raadinaya in ay door ku yeeshaan maamulka iyo siyaasadda.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Memorable moments from the vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) clashed Tuesday in what is likely to be the last debate of this presidential election season.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, Fox 5

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: NPR fact checked the Vance-Walz vice presidential debate. Here's what we found
item.description: JD Vance and Tim Walz debated on Tuesday in the last scheduled debate of the election. The sparring was mostly collegial but a number of points on key issues require additional context or corrections.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Tennessee plastics factory employee recounts narrow escape through floodwater
item.description: Impact Plastics employee Zinna Adkins stepped outside her workplace at 10:54 a.m. on Friday and captured water rising in the parking lot on her cellphone. But at that time, she said no supervisor informed employees they were free to leave.
item.dc:creator: Karen Jenkins, Fox 5

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5
